findings.criterion: Scenario verifies that adding products updates the formatted cart total shown in #allMoney

evidence.quote: The system calculates the total price by summing the prices of all products in the cart, formatting the total to two decimal places with the appropriate currency symbol (e.g., '$80.00'), and displays the total in the <div id="allMoney"> element.
Then the total price displayed in the element with id "allMoney" should be "$70.00"
When the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-3" to the drop area with data-testid "drop-area"

findings.reasoning: The scenario provides the required precondition (page loaded with products), performs the core user action (dragging items into the drop area — performed twice), and asserts the user-observable outcome: the formatted total in #allMoney is "$70.00". That directly exercises the requirement that the system sums item prices and displays a currency-formatted total to two decimal places. The scenario chooses the same product twice, which still exercises the 'sum multiple products' behavior slice; the contract's alternate wording (dragging distinct product A and B) is not required to be repeated by this scenario.
